title: Dashboard Shell — tkinter Layout
description: Create a DashboardWindow class wrapping Tk with: a menu bar (File menu with Exit, View menu with panel options, Tools menu with Search, Help menu with About), a left sidebar Frame with navigation buttons (Messages, Tasks, Files, System), a main content_frame (Frame) for panel content, and a status_bar Label at the bottom. Use grid geometry manager. Include a switch_panel(panel_name) method that clears the content_frame and updates the status bar. Store references to the menu_bar, sidebar, content_frame, and status_bar as attributes.
test_code:
# Tests for Step 8: DashboardWindow
import sys

assert 'DashboardWindow' in dir(), 'DashboardWindow class must be defined'

# Try to test with tkinter (may not be available in headless environments)
try:
    import tkinter as tk
    # Create a hidden root for testing
    root = tk.Tk()
    root.withdraw()  # Hide the window

    dashboard = DashboardWindow(root)

    # Test that key attributes exist
    assert hasattr(dashboard, 'menu_bar'), 'Must have menu_bar attribute'
    assert hasattr(dashboard, 'sidebar'), 'Must have sidebar attribute'
    assert hasattr(dashboard, 'content_frame'), 'Must have content_frame attribute'
    assert hasattr(dashboard, 'status_bar'), 'Must have status_bar attribute'
    assert hasattr(dashboard, 'root'), 'Must have root attribute'

    # Test menu_bar is a Menu widget
    assert isinstance(dashboard.menu_bar, tk.Menu), 'menu_bar must be a tk.Menu'

    # Test sidebar is a Frame
    assert isinstance(dashboard.sidebar, tk.Frame), 'sidebar must be a tk.Frame'

    # Test content_frame is a Frame
    assert isinstance(dashboard.content_frame, tk.Frame), 'content_frame must be a tk.Frame'

    # Test status_bar is a Label
    assert isinstance(dashboard.status_bar, tk.Label), 'status_bar must be a tk.Label'

    # Test switch_panel
    assert callable(getattr(dashboard, 'switch_panel', None)), 'Must have switch_panel method'
    dashboard.switch_panel('Tasks')
    assert dashboard._current_panel == 'Tasks', 'switch_panel should update _current_panel'

    # Test that status bar updates
    status_text = dashboard.status_bar.cget('text')
    assert 'Tasks' in status_text or 'task' in status_text.lower(), \
        f'Status bar should mention Tasks, got: {status_text}'

    # Test switching to another panel
    dashboard.switch_panel('Messages')
    assert dashboard._current_panel == 'Messages', 'Should switch to Messages'

    # Test that sidebar has buttons (check children)
    sidebar_children = dashboard.sidebar.winfo_children()
    assert len(sidebar_children) >= 4, f'Sidebar should have at least 4 buttons, got {len(sidebar_children)}'

    # Cleanup
    root.destroy()

except ImportError:
    # tkinter not available, test the class structure only
    dashboard = DashboardWindow.__new__(DashboardWindow)
    assert callable(getattr(DashboardWindow, 'switch_panel', None)), 'Must have switch_panel method'
    assert callable(getattr(DashboardWindow, '_create_menu_bar', None)), 'Must have _create_menu_bar'
    assert callable(getattr(DashboardWindow, '_create_sidebar', None)), 'Must have _create_sidebar'
    assert callable(getattr(DashboardWindow, '_create_content_area', None)), 'Must have _create_content_area'
    assert callable(getattr(DashboardWindow, '_create_status_bar', None)), 'Must have _create_status_bar'
acceptance_criteria: DashboardWindow wraps a Tk root window
Menu bar has File (with Exit), View, Tools (with Search), Help (with About) menus
Sidebar Frame contains navigation buttons: Messages, Tasks, Files, System
content_frame is a Frame in the main area for panel content
status_bar is a Label at the bottom showing current state
switch_panel() clears content_frame children and updates status bar
All widgets use grid geometry manager
Attributes menu_bar, sidebar, content_frame, status_bar are stored
hints: Create the root with tk.Tk() if not provided; call root.title('Command Center').
Use root.columnconfigure(1, weight=1) and root.rowconfigure(0, weight=1) so the content area expands.
For switch_panel, destroy all children: for w in self.content_frame.winfo_children(): w.destroy()
sidebar buttons: tk.Button(self.sidebar, text='Tasks', command=lambda: self.switch_panel('Tasks'))
